title: Executive Order 5149—Authorizing the Reinstatement of Frances D. Haldeman Without Regard to Civil Service Rules
text:
Mrs. Frances D. Haldeman may be reinstated as an operative in the Bureau of Engraving and Printing without regard to the length of her separation from the service.
Mrs. Haldeman was a classified printer's assistant in 1908 and 1909. She has passed both the junior file clerk and the general clerical examinations. She is the widow of Frank Haldeman, a plate printer in the Bureau of Engraving and Printing, who died December 14, 1927, after a service of 21 years, leaving Mrs. Haldeman and a dependent daughter of 17 years without means of support.
The Secretary of the Treasury recommends this Order.
HERBERT HOOVER
The White House,
July 5, 1929.
